mission.title: Install and Configure KubeAI
mission.description: KubeAI is an AI Inference Operator for Kubernetes designed to serve machine learning models, including LLMs and embeddings, in production environments. It simplifies the deployment and scaling of AI models while optimizing performance through intelligent routing and caching mechanisms. Key trade-offs include the need for adequate hardware resources and the potential complexity of managing model configurations.

steps.title: Add the KubeAI Helm repository
steps.description:
This step adds the KubeAI Helm chart repository to your local Helm configuration, allowing you to install KubeAI easily.

```bash

helm repo add kubeai https://charts.kubeai.org

helm repo update

```

> This URL points to the official KubeAI Helm chart repository.
> Updates your local Helm chart repository cache.

steps.title: Install KubeAI CRDs
steps.description:
This step installs the Custom Resource Definitions (CRDs) required for KubeAI to function properly.

```bash

kubectl apply -f https://raw.githubusercontent.com/substratusai/kubeai/helm-chart-kubeai-0.23.1/deploy/crds/kubeai_v1alpha1_model_crd.yaml

kubectl apply -f https://raw.githubusercontent.com/substratusai/kubeai/helm-chart-kubeai-0.23.1/deploy/crds/kubeai_v1alpha1_inference_crd.yaml

```

> This command installs the Model CRD.
> This command installs the Inference CRD.

steps.title: Install KubeAI using Helm
steps.description:
This step installs the KubeAI operator in the specified namespace, allowing you to manage AI models.

```bash

helm install kubeai kubeai/kubeai --namespace kubeai --create-namespace --version 0.23.1

```

> Installs KubeAI in the 'kubeai' namespace.

steps.title: Verify KubeAI installation
steps.description:
This step checks if the KubeAI pods are running correctly in the cluster.

```bash

kubectl get pods -n kubeai

```

> You should see KubeAI pods in the Running state.

steps.title: Create an example Inference resource
steps.description:
This step creates an example Inference resource to test KubeAI functionality.

```bash

kubectl apply -f - <<EOF
apiVersion: kubeai.substratus.ai/v1alpha1
kind: Inference
metadata:
  name: example-inference
  namespace: kubeai
spec:
  model: example-model
EOF

```

> This creates a sample inference resource.
commands:
kubectl apply -f - <<EOF
apiVersion: kubeai.substratus.ai/v1alpha1
kind: Inference
metadata:
  name: example-inference
  namespace: kubeai
spec:
  model: example-model
EOF

steps.title: Verify the Inference resource
steps.description:
This step checks if the Inference resource is ready.

```bash

kubectl get inference -n kubeai

```

> You should see the Inference resource with a status of Ready.

uninstall.title: Uninstall KubeAI
uninstall.description:
This step removes KubeAI and its associated resources. Be aware that this may lead to data loss of any models or inferences created.

```bash

helm uninstall kubeai --namespace kubeai

kubectl delete namespace kubeai

```

> This command uninstalls the KubeAI operator.
> This command deletes the namespace and all resources within it.

upgrade.title: Upgrade KubeAI
upgrade.description:
Before upgrading, ensure you back up any critical data. This step will upgrade KubeAI to the latest version.

```bash

kubectl get inference -n kubeai -o yaml > backup-inference.yaml

helm upgrade kubeai kubeai/kubeai --namespace kubeai --version 0.23.1

kubectl get pods -n kubeai

```

> Backup current inference resources.
> Upgrades KubeAI to the specified version.
> Verify that the pods are running after the upgrade.
commands: kubectl get inference -n kubeai -o yaml > backup-inference.yaml

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'Failed to create Inference resource'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** The Inference resource may not be properly defined or the model is not available.

**Fix:**
Check the model definition and ensure it exists in the cluster.

troubleshooting.title: Pod is in 'CrashLoopBackOff' state
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** The KubeAI pod may be failing due to misconfiguration or resource limits.

**Fix:**
Check the pod logs using 'kubectl logs <pod-name> -n kubeai' for more details.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'No resources found'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** The specified namespace may not exist or the resources have been deleted.

**Fix:**
Ensure the namespace 'kubeai' exists and resources are created.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'Unable to connect to the server'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** The Kubernetes API server may be down or the kubeconfig is misconfigured.

**Fix:**
Check your Kubernetes cluster status and kubeconfig settings.

versionNotes.changes: Added support for new model types and improved load balancing algorithms.
versionNotes.migrationSteps: No specific migration steps required for this version.

resolution.summary: You should see the Inference resource with a status of Ready when everything is working: 'example-inference Ready'.

EOF

prerequisites.description: Ensure you have a Kubernetes cluster running with the specified resources.
